title: Version string 1.3.0rc4 while CHANGELOG heading is released [1.3.0]

title: gitleaks full-history reports 3 non-baselined hits (synthetic AWS fixtures echoed into prior run artifacts)
evidence: local 'gitleaks detect' full history reports 3 aws-access-token hits, all in workflow-artifacts/release-review/20260720-125929/ (03-findings-register.csv, 12-final-response.md, section-summaries/02-...md); same synthetic AKIA fixture strings already baselined for tests/test_ocman.py; NOT live secrets (nothing to rotate); secret-scan.yml CI currently green

title: reconnect/kill process-signalling surface traced; hardening confirmed adequate (no defect)
evidence: Read _reuse_guard_ok/_kill_pid_gracefully/_pid_is_gone/cli_reconnect/cli_kill: own-user check via /proc/<pid> st_uid + _pid_looks_like_opencode re-validated immediately before EACH signal; zombie-aware gone check; require_opencode() up front in reconnect; survivor -> STOP no-exec; one confirmation; dry-run/no = zero side effects; narrow TOCTOU window acknowledged in docstrings
impact: No defect: the highest-risk LIVE surface in the 1.3.0 cycle (signalling the wrong process) is guarded correctly. Recorded as traced-and-clear per mandatory LIVE review

title: doctor/list-running server-auth check traced; no password leakage (no defect)
evidence: Read _server_password_env_state/_probe_app_auth/_bind_is_loopback: reads OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD STATE only (never value/log); owner-only /proc reads; probe only own loopback listeners never user-supplied/non-loopback; read-only GET /app; fails to 'unknown' never a false all-clear
impact: No defect: security-sensitive new code handles auth state without leaking secrets and fails safe

title: db_rename_session correctness traced; bound params + txn rollback + finally-close (no defect)
evidence: Read db_rename_session: BEGIN/commit/rollback, new_title as bound param (no injection), not-found -> rollback+RecoveryError, connection closed in finally on all paths

evidence: reconnect (reuse-guard/zombie/other-user/partial-kill/missing-opencode/dry-run/no/zero-match/nothing-to-resume), kill (reap-real-child/reuse-guard/ignores-SIGTERM/force-SIGKILL/survivor-nonzero/e2e/dry-run/zero-match/pattern-own-user), rename (id/substring/to-preserve/empty-reject/dry-run/running-guard), doctor server check (vulnerable-ERROR/exposed-WARN/authed-OK/none-OK/detection-error-UNKNOWN/all-users+probe/wired), filters (lp/ls/lr/empty-json/alias), _git_decisions_for_state branches, storage worker mount guard, prune worker error path; 473 passed 2 skipped

title: ARCHITECTURE.md top-level-verbs enumeration omits reconnect/kill/rename (and lr)
evidence: ARCHITECTURE.md:20-22 explicitly enumerates top-level verbs (search info disk logs spend running doctor reclaim models compaction-prompt filter move export ui help) but omits the 1.3.0 additions reconnect, kill, rename
impact: A cold-start engineer reading ARCHITECTURE gets an incomplete command map; low harm (README is authoritative reference) but the enumeration is now stale
recommended_action: Add reconnect/kill/rename to the enumerated list in Section 7

title: DECISIONS.md has no entry for the reconnect/kill process-signalling safety model
evidence: DECISIONS.md records significant cross-cutting decisions (linking IPDs); the reconnect+kill safety model (own-user-only via /proc st_uid, PID-reuse guard, zombie-aware, foreground-exec-inherits-env, no bare relaunch, Linux-only, SIGTERM-then-optional-SIGKILL) is a significant safety decision with rejected alternatives, but has no DECISIONS.md entry (only per-feature executed IPDs)
impact: A no-context engineer learns the signalling safety rationale only by reading two IPDs; DECISIONS.md is the intended home for cross-cutting safety decisions
recommended_action: Append one dated DECISIONS.md entry summarizing the signalling safety model + linking the reconnect/kill IPDs
validation: Author in Section 7 (Low RR; conversation-recovered intent verifiable against code)
